meanings.usage: 不可数名词，强调独处的状态，可含褒义（享受独处）或贬义（孤单）
常用搭配lure sb. to/into sth.，指用具有吸引力的事物引导某人做某事
指吸引他人的事物或钓鱼等用的诱饵
表示天生的本能时为不可数，表具体的直觉反应时为可数，常用搭配by instinct（出于本能）
指休闲散步的人，来自动词ramble（漫步、闲逛）
不可数名词，强调对未来的预判和规划能力，反义词为hindsight（事后聪明）
常用搭配rig up，指快速或临时搭建、装配设备等结构
可指空间上的向下行进，也可指血统、身份的传承，反义词为ascent（上升）
常用复数形式，搭配take precautions against sth.（采取措施预防某事）
过去式和过去分词均为foretold，强调提前预告尚未发生的事
既可指地理上的巨大裂口，也可引申为观点、群体之间的巨大分歧
可指物品的瑕疵、人的性格缺点或结构上的裂隙，常用搭配flaw in sth.
地理上指高原，也可引申为发展、增长停滞的平稳阶段，复数形式为plateaus或plateaux
常用搭配wade through sth.，既指蹚过水域，也引申为吃力地完成冗长的工作
用来形容气氛、场景等让人感到不安、诡异的，侧重超自然的怪异感
常指湿润或光滑的表面反射光线，多接介词with表示因某物而闪亮

phrases.usage: 表示逐渐被熟知、被称为，强调过程性而非突然成名

examples.en: Cave exploration, or pot-holing, as it has come to be known, is a relatively new sport.
examples.zh: 洞穴勘探，也就是人们现在常说的洞穴探险，是一项相对较新的运动。

examples.en: The small town has come to be known for its well-preserved ancient buildings.
examples.zh: 这个小镇现在已经因保存完好的古建筑而广为人知。

phrases.usage: 指临时或快速搭建、装配设备、结构等

examples.en: It can take as long as eight days to rig up rope ladders and to establish supply bases before a descent can be made into a very deep cave.
examples.zh: 在进入非常深的洞穴之前，可能需要长达8天的时间来搭建绳梯并建立补给基地。

examples.en: The hikers rigged up a simple shelter with branches and a plastic sheet when it started to rain.
examples.zh: 开始下雨时，徒步旅行者们用树枝和塑料布临时搭了一个简易庇护所。

phrases.usage: 指小心翼翼地沿某条路径慢慢移动，强调动作的谨慎

examples.en: They had to edge their way along this, sometimes wading across shallow streams, or swimming across deep pools.
examples.zh: 他们不得不沿着这条狭窄的通道慢慢挪行，时而蹚过浅溪，时而游过深潭。

examples.en: We had to edge our way along the crowded platform to get to the train door.
examples.zh: 我们不得不沿着拥挤的站台慢慢挪到列车车门处。

phrases.usage: 既可指纵身跳入水中，也可引申为突然投入、开始做某事

examples.en: They plunged into the lake, and after loading their gear on an inflatable rubber dinghy, let the current carry them to the other side.
examples.zh: 他们跳入湖中，把装备装上充气橡皮艇后，就让水流带着他们漂向对岸。

examples.en: After graduation, he plunged into his first job with great enthusiasm.
examples.zh: 毕业后，他满怀热情地投入了自己的第一份工作。

phrases.usage: 指用力挤过狭窄的空间，强调空间的狭小和通过的费力

examples.en: Squeezing through a cleft in the rocks, the pot-holers arrived at an enormous cavern, the size of a huge concert hall.
examples.zh: 探险者们挤过岩石的缝隙，来到了一个足有大型音乐厅那么大的巨大洞穴里。

examples.en: There was only a small gap in the fence, so we had to squeeze through it.
examples.zh: 围栏上只有一个小缺口，所以我们不得不挤过去。

phrases.usage: 表示激发、唤起本能，属于常用的动宾搭配

examples.en: They arouse instincts which can only be dimly understood.
examples.zh: 它们激发了人们的本能，而这种本能只能被模糊地理解。

examples.en: The smell of cooking aroused the dog's hunting instincts immediately.
examples.zh: 烹饪的气味立刻唤起了这只狗的狩猎本能。

phrases.usage: 通常指水流将某物冲到岸边或某个地方，也可表示餐后清洗餐具

examples.en: At the far end of the lake, they came to huge piles of rubble which had been washed up by the water.
examples.zh: 在湖的尽头，他们看到了被水冲上来的大堆碎石。

examples.en: After the storm, a lot of driftwood was washed up on the beach.
examples.zh: 风暴过后，很多浮木被冲到了海滩上。

grammar.title: It引导的强调句
grammar.definition: 用来突出句子中的某个成分（主语、宾语、状语等），强调该成分是动作发生的核心原因或主体，基本含义为“正是……”。
grammar.structure: It is/was + 被强调部分 + that/who + 句子剩余部分
grammar.usage: 当被强调部分指人时可以用who，其他情况一律用that；去掉It is/was和that/who后句子结构依然完整。

examples.en: Perhaps it is the desire for solitude or the chance of making an unexpected discovery that lures people down to the depths of the earth.
examples.zh: 也许正是对独处的渴望，或是获得意外发现的机会，吸引着人们深入地下深处。

examples.en: It was his dedication to the project that helped the team achieve success finally.
examples.zh: 正是他对这个项目的付出，帮助团队最终取得了成功。

grammar.title: 虚拟条件句的倒装
grammar.definition: 当虚拟条件句的谓语含有were, should, had时，可以省略if，将这些词移到句首形成倒装，用于书面语中，表达与事实相反的假设。
grammar.structure: Were/Should/Had + 主语 + 谓语剩余部分，主句
grammar.usage: 多用于正式文体，否定词not不能提前，需放在主语之后。

examples.en: The cave might never have been discovered had not the entrance been spotted by the distinguished French pot-holer, Berger.
examples.zh: 若不是法国著名洞穴探险家伯杰发现了这个洞口，这个洞穴可能永远不会被人知晓。

examples.en: Had you followed my advice earlier, you would not have made such a mistake.
examples.zh: 要是你早点听我的建议，你就不会犯这样的错误了。

grammar.title: 定语从句的分隔现象
grammar.definition: 定语从句通常紧跟在先行词之后，但有时为了平衡句子结构，会被其他成分（如状语、谓语等）分隔开，需要根据语义判断先行词。
grammar.structure: 先行词 + 其他成分 + 关系词 + 定语从句
grammar.usage: 需通过语义和逻辑确定定语从句修饰的对象，避免误判先行词。

examples.en: In this part of the cave, they could hear an insistent booming sound which they found was caused by a small waterspout shooting down into a pool from the roof of the cave.
examples.zh: 在洞穴的这一部分，他们能听到持续的轰鸣声，后来他们发现这声音是从洞穴顶部倾泻到水池里的小水柱发出的。

examples.en: A new teacher will come tomorrow who will teach you history.
examples.zh: 明天会来一位新老师，他将教你们历史。

sentencePatterns.pattern: It is + 形容词 + to do sth.

original.en: It is impossible to give a satisfactory explanation for a pot-holer's motives.
original.zh: 要对洞穴探险者的动机给出满意的解释是不可能的。

imitations.en: It is necessary to learn basic safety rules before starting the experiment.
imitations.zh: 在开始实验之前学习基本的安全规则是很有必要的。

imitations.en: It is exciting to travel to a place you have never visited before.
imitations.zh: 去一个你从未去过的地方旅行是很令人兴奋的。

sentencePatterns.pattern: sth. have the same + 名词 + which/that sth. else have for sb.

original.en: For him, caves have the same peculiar fascination which high mountains have for the climber.
original.zh: 对他来说，洞穴有着独特的魅力，就像高山对登山者的魅力一样。

imitations.en: For children, fairy tales have the same magic attraction which advanced technology has for engineers.
imitations.zh: 对孩子们来说，童话故事有着神奇的吸引力，就像先进科技对工程师的吸引力一样。

imitations.en: For painters, natural scenery has the same profound inspiration which classic music has for musicians.
imitations.zh: 对画家来说，自然风景有着深厚的启发意义，就像古典音乐对音乐家的启发一样。

sentencePatterns.pattern: After doing sth., sb. do sth. until + 从句

original.en: After entering the narrow gap on the plateau, they climbed down the steep sides of the cave until they came to a narrow corridor.
original.zh: 进入高原上的狭窄洞口后，他们沿着洞穴陡峭的岩壁往下爬，直到来到一条狭窄的通道前。

imitations.en: After packing all the camping equipment, we walked along the mountain path until we found a flat clearing.
imitations.zh: 收拾好所有露营装备后，我们沿着山间小路前行，直到找到了一片平坦的空地。

imitations.en: After finishing his daily homework, the boy read storybooks until his mother asked him to sleep.
imitations.zh: 完成日常作业后，这个男孩一直在读故事书，直到妈妈叫他睡觉。

sentencePatterns.pattern: the only sound that could be heard was made by + 名词

original.en: In the eerie silence of the cavern, the only sound that could be heard was made by water which dripped continuously from the high dome above them.
original.zh: 在洞穴诡异的寂静中，唯一能听到的声音是从他们头顶高高的穹顶不断滴落的水声。

imitations.en: In the quiet library, the only sound that could be heard was made by pages being turned gently.
imitations.zh: 在安静的图书馆里，唯一能听到的声音是轻轻翻书的声音。

imitations.en: In the empty street at midnight, the only sound that could be heard was made by the cold wind blowing through the trees.
imitations.zh: 午夜空荡荡的街道上，唯一能听到的是寒风吹过树林的声音。
